deployedGeneratedSources.name: #utility.yul

documentation.text: @notice The administrator who deployed the contract.
@notice Running total of records created; also used as the next record ID.
@notice A single medical record owned by one patient.
@notice Stored records, keyed by record ID.

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "Only owner can perform this action"

arguments.value: Only owner can perform this action

argumentTypes.typeString: literal_string "Only owner can perform this action"

devdoc.details: An administrator (the contract owner) registers patients and doctors.      Each patient owns their records and decides which registered doctors      are allowed to read them. Doctors can only view a record after the      patient has explicitly granted access. SECURITY: Integer overflow / underflow protection is built in.           This contract targets Solidity ^0.8.x, where all arithmetic           (e.g. recordCounter++) reverts automatically on overflow,           so no SafeMath library is required.

addRecord(string,string).details: Only callable by a registered patient. Diagnosis and treatment      must be non-empty. recordCounter increments first, then the record      is stored (state change) and an event is emitted.

returns._0: True if the doctor is permitted, false otherwise.

grantAccess(address).details: Only callable by a registered patient; the doctor must be registered.

params.doctor: The doctor address to grant access to.

registerDoctor(address).details: Reverts on the zero address or an already-registered doctor.

params.doctorAddress: The wallet address to register as a doctor.

registerPatient(address).details: Reverts on the zero address or an already-registered patient.      SECURITY: input validation via require() with descriptive messages.

params.patientAddress: The wallet address to register as a patient.

requestAccess(address).details: Emits an event so the patient can later grant access. Does not      change permissions by itself.

params.patient: The patient the doctor is requesting access from.

revokeAccess(address).details: Only callable by a registered patient; the doctor must be registered.

params.doctor: The doctor address to revoke access from.

viewRecord(uint256).details: SECURITY: access control on read — the caller must be either the      patient who owns the record or a doctor the patient has authorised.      Reverts if the record does not exist.

params.recordId: The ID of the record to view.

returns._0: The record ID, diagnosis, treatment, patient address, and timestamp.

devdoc.title: MedicalRecord

addRecord(string,string).notice: Add a medical record for the calling patient.

checkAccess(address,address).notice: Check whether a doctor currently has access to a patient's records.

constructor.notice: Sets the deploying account as the owner/administrator.

grantAccess(address).notice: Grant a registered doctor permission to view the caller's records.

owner().notice: The administrator who deployed the contract.

recordCounter().notice: Running total of records created; also used as the next record ID.

records(uint256).notice: Stored records, keyed by record ID.

registerDoctor(address).notice: Register a new doctor. Owner only.

registerPatient(address).notice: Register a new patient. Owner only.

requestAccess(address).notice: A registered doctor requests access to a patient's records.

revokeAccess(address).notice: Revoke a doctor's permission to view the caller's records.

viewRecord(uint256).notice: View a medical record by ID.

userdoc.notice: A patient-controlled platform for sharing medical records on Ethereum.
